summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed drone incursions over Odesa, Chornomorsk, and Kharkiv, while a night attack damaged the Saratov oil refinery in Russia. A Ukrainian drone struck the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, marking the first known targeted attack on such equipment. The 3rd Army Corps announced control over logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk, extending Ukrainian UAV influence to several towns.
key_points: Ukrainian Shahed drones entered Odesa airspace, with 11 observed over the Black Sea and additional strikes over Chornomorsk and Kharkiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67317).

A night attack on the Saratov oil refinery caused fires and damage to the primary processing unit ELU-AVT-6, affecting a plant processing ~6 million tonnes of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
Ukrainian UAVs hit the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, the first targeted attack on core NPP equipment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
The 3rd Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes in Luhansk, reaching 205 km into enemy‑held territory and securing towns including Luhansk and Starobilsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Russia warned of a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
Russian TASS reported the liberation of 63 settlements during the spring 2026 campaign (Source: @tass_agency/378348).
Ukrainian drones attacked a logistics company depot in Dnipro, igniting warehouses and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian Shahed drones penetrated Odesa and surrounding areas, while a night strike damaged the Saratov refinery in Russia. A Ukrainian UAV struck the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant’s machine hall, a first‑ever targeted attack on such infrastructure. The 3rd Army Corps extended control over logistics routes deep into occupied Luhansk, securing multiple towns.

points: Ukrainian Shahed drones were observed over Odesa, with 11 reported from the Black Sea, and additional Shahed activity over Chornomorsk (3) and Kharkiv (1) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67317).

The 3rd Army Corps announced control of logistics routes in Luhansk, reaching 205 km into enemy‑held territory and claiming towns such as Luhansk, Starobilsk, Alchevsk, Bryanka, and Kadiivka (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, marking the first known targeted attack on core NPP equipment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
A Ukrainian drone destroyed a postal service depot in Dnipro, causing the building to burn completely, with no casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Ukrainian forces reported a night attack on the Saratov refinery, causing fires and damage to the ELU‑AVT‑6 processing unit (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
Russia warned of a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
TASS reported that Russian forces liberated 63 settlements during the spring 2026 campaign (Source: @tass_agency/378348).
A Russian statement indicated no casualties and normal operation after the Ukrainian strike on the Zaporizhzhia plant on 30 May (Source: @tass_agency/378311).
Russian officials noted a stable cholera situation domestically, unrelated to the conflict (Source: @tass_agency/378331).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were sighted over Kharkiv, with one reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near the border of Sumy and Kharkiv regions, heading toward Poltava direction (Source: @kpszsu/63407).
Ukrainian drones attacked a logistics depot in Dnipro, igniting warehouses and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed over the northern part of Chernihiv region along the Belarus border, heading west (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
Reactive UAVs were reported along the northern Chernihiv border with Belarus, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
UAV groups were observed on the north‑west of Chernihiv region along the Belarus border, also heading west (Source: @kpszsu/63428).
UAVs were noted on the north‑west of Kharkiv region with changing course (Source: @kpszsu/63438).
UAVs from Kyiv region were reported moving toward Zhytomyr (Source: @kpszsu/63406).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were reported over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, with 2 observed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67318).
UAV groups were noted moving from the south‑east of Dnipro city, with variable course (Source: @kpszsu/63418).
UAVs were reported from the south‑west of Dnipro, heading north (Source: @kpszsu/63413).
UAVs from the south‑west of Kharkiv region were observed with southern and western courses (Source: @kpszsu/63432).
Multiple Shahed drone incursions were reported over Odesa, Chornomorsk, and Kharkiv, indicating sustained UAV pressure from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67317, @mon1tor_ua/67318).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant’s machine hall, a first‑ever targeted attack on such infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Saratov oil refinery, causing fires and damage to key processing equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
Russian air defense reported no casualties from the May 30 strike on Zaporizhzhia, stating the plant remains operational (Source: @tass_agency/378311).

key_developments: Ukrainian Shahed drones entered Odesa airspace, with 11 observed over the Black Sea and additional strikes over Chornomorsk and Kharkiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67317).

A night attack on the Saratov oil refinery caused fires and damage to the primary processing unit ELU‑AVT‑6, affecting a plant processing ~6 million tonnes of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
Ukrainian UAVs hit the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, the first targeted attack on core NPP equipment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
The 3rd Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes in Luhansk, reaching 205 km into enemy‑held territory and securing towns including Luhansk and Starobilsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Russia warned of a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
TASS reported the liberation of 63 settlements during the spring 2026 Russian campaign (Source: @tass_agency/378348).
Ukrainian drones attacked a logistics depot in Dnipro, igniting warehouses and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV activity remains intense, targeting critical infrastructure such as the Zaporizhzhia plant and the Saratov refinery, while also penetrating deep into occupied Luhansk logistics corridors. Russian threats of Iskander‑M missile launches from Bryansk add a high‑risk ballistic dimension to the southern front. The continued Shahed incursions over Odesa and Kharkiv suggest persistent aerial pressure from the Black Sea, challenging both air defense and civilian safety.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command structures, notably the 3rd Army Corps, are expanding UAV‑based control over supply routes, aiming to disrupt Russian logistics deep within occupied territories. Russian statements emphasize defensive readiness against missile threats and highlight territorial gains, indicating a focus on consolidating liberated areas. International observers note the unprecedented nature of the Zaporizhzhia machine‑hall strike, raising concerns about nuclear safety protocols.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed drone launch vectors from the Black Sea is critical for early warning in Odesa and surrounding regions. The damage to Saratov's ELU‑AVT‑6 unit may impact Russian fuel supply chains for both domestic and export markets. The 205 km reach of Ukrainian UAV operations into Luhansk suggests enhanced command‑and‑control capabilities and potential for further deep‑strike missions.
